invariants.statement: Sovereignty is consciousness becoming capable of action: not only a control right but a pathway of consciousness — the growing capacity to perceive, choose, create, delegate, repair, and steward with awareness and responsibility. Agency without awareness acts without understanding consequence, and power without accountability creates harm, so sovereignty requires conscious agency, not merely control. This is why metaMe does not merely serve the user; metaMe develops the person. The purpose is not to make humans dependent on smarter machines but to help humans become more sovereign in a world of agents.

provenance.source: ES Paper 5 'The Experience Polity', §22 (Sovereignty as consciousness capable of action) + ES Paper 4 §1 + §29 (metaMe develops the person) — new; the developmental/consciousness telos of the series, extending inv.polity.203 (the economy of the sovereign self) toward human development. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Experience Sovereignty canonization).

invariants.statement: The Internet has crossed the threshold from infrastructure to society: we built a network and got a society, and societies require constitutions. The constitutional response is not another platform, network, or operating system but a constitutional (civic) layer above today's transport and application layers — one that constitutionalises the existing Internet rather than replacing it, where identity is constitutional rather than platform-defined, authority is explicit rather than assumed, rights are protected rather than granted by Terms of Service, and governance belongs to citizens as well as platforms.

provenance.source: Thresholds — 'The Internet Needs a Constitution' + Constitutional Seed 1 — new foundational claim (the Constitutional Internet as a civic layer above the transport layer); no prior crystal invariant carries it. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Thresholds / Constitutional Internet canonization).

invariants.statement: Every generation crosses a threshold into a new civic order — a shift that has already occurred before it is named. The Constitutional Internet is the threshold from an Internet organized around platforms and identities to one organized around agency, standing, and constitutional participation; a constitutional response follows a threshold already crossed, it does not predict one.

provenance.source: Thresholds — Constitutional Seed 9 'The Threshold' + Thresholds Editorial Canon — new meta-principle (the Threshold framing); no prior crystal invariant carries it. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Thresholds / Constitutional Internet canonization).

invariants.statement: Personhood precedes identity. Every constitutional subject first exists as a unique entity; identity is not the beginning of participation but merely one way an entity becomes socially recognizable, invoked when necessary for coordination. The Constitutional Internet begins with personhood and agency, not identity — most interactions require neither identification nor disclosure.

provenance.source: Thresholds — Constitutional Seed 2 'Entity Before Identity' + Constitutional Brief — extends inv.polity.175 (personhood precedes permission) into the entity-vs-identity distinction the crystal does not yet carry. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Thresholds / Constitutional Internet canonization).

invariants.statement: Healthy civilisations are built on the hierarchy Ethics → Law → Economics → Consequence → Code: code should never define law, law should constrain code, consequence should govern code, and law itself should be grounded in ethics. The platform Internet inverted this order — code shaped economics, economics shaped law, and ethics were left to catch up — making software the de facto constitution. 'Code is law' gets the order exactly backwards.

provenance.source: Thresholds — 'The Internet Needs a Constitution' ('Code Became Law') — new claim (the Ethics→Law→Economics→Consequence→Code hierarchy and its platform-era inversion). Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Thresholds / Constitutional Internet canonization).

invariants.statement: Constitutional vacuums never remain empty. When constitutional principles are absent, something else fills the vacuum: on the platform Internet, software, Terms of Service, algorithms, and private capital assumed constitutional functions — who may participate, what may be said, how identity and reputation are established, who is visible or excluded — without being governed by constitutional principles, producing a technocratic plutocracy by architecture rather than intent.

provenance.source: Thresholds — 'The Internet Needs a Constitution' ('Nature Abhors a Constitutional Vacuum') — new claim (constitutional vacuum → capital-governed constitutional functions). Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Thresholds / Constitutional Internet canonization).

invariants.statement: The evolution of AI unfolds in three stages, each posing a different question: can machines think? (intelligence), can machines act? (agency), and can machines legitimately represent us? (representation). The move from 'Can AI do this?' to 'Can AI do this for me?' is the transition from intelligence to agency; the defining challenge of the agentic era is the third stage — representation, whose test is legitimacy, not capability.

provenance.source: Thresholds — 'The Missing Primitive of the AI Revolution' ('Intelligence. Agency. Representation.') — new staged claim; complements the bounded-delegation cluster (inv.constitutional.014/015, inv.polity.170/183). Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Thresholds / Constitutional Internet canonization).

invariants.statement: Representation is fundamentally different from intelligence: it requires authority, accountability, governance, and evidence. Without those four, a digital representative may be capable but cannot be legitimate. The missing primitive of the agentic Internet is therefore not another model, benchmark, or context window but governance — legitimacy, not capability.

provenance.source: Thresholds — 'The Missing Primitive of the AI Revolution' — new claim (legitimacy is the missing primitive); extends bounded delegation inv.constitutional.014/015 and inv.polity.170/183. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Thresholds / Constitutional Internet canonization).

invariants.statement: Trust does not scale; governance does. The future of digital representation is built not on trusting platforms, providers, operating systems, or infrastructure but on verifiable governance: instead of trusting software, participants trust policy — what a representative may do, for whom, under whose authority, within which limits, requiring which approvals, meeting which Standing requirements. The representative does not invent those rules; its principal does, and it simply carries them.

provenance.source: Thresholds — 'The Missing Primitive of the AI Revolution' ('From Trusting Platforms to Governing Representation') — new claim (trust doesn't scale, governance does); extends inv.polity.170/173. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Thresholds / Constitutional Internet canonization).

invariants.statement: The Polity Passport is the constitutional credential of the Constitutional Internet and a portable governance framework for bounded digital representation. Where identity answers 'who are you?' and Standing answers 'can you be trusted?', the Passport answers the distinct question 'what are you authorised to do?' — binding authority to policy, policy to accountability, accountability to evidence, and evidence to independently verifiable, privacy-preserving receipts. It lets people participate, build Standing, and exercise rights without disclosing identity except where genuinely necessary.

provenance.source: Thresholds — 'The Missing Primitive of the AI Revolution' ('The Polity Passport') + Constitutional Seed 7 — new claim (Passport as the authorization credential, distinct from identity and Standing). Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Thresholds / Constitutional Internet canonization).

invariants.statement: Every significant action by a digital representative can produce a cryptographically verifiable receipt that proves what occurred without exposing the underlying private information, and those receipts belong to the principal, not the platform. Accountability need never require surveillance, transparency need never require exposure, and verification need never require surrendering privacy — privacy and accountability reinforce rather than undermine one another.

provenance.source: Thresholds — 'The Missing Primitive of the AI Revolution' + Constitutional Seed 3 'Privacy with Accountability' — reaffirms the privacy-with-accountability and DVN-receipt canon; extends it with the new 'receipts belong to the principal, not the platform' and privacy/accountability-reinforce claims. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Thresholds / Constitutional Internet canonization).

invariants.statement: In an economy of digital representatives, trust becomes reciprocal: the question is no longer only 'can I trust my representative?' but 'can my representative trust yours?'. When representatives negotiate, exchange credentials, transact, and coordinate, every participant must verify not only identity but authority — who delegated it, whether it is still valid or revoked, and whether the representative satisfies the required Standing. This makes agents legitimate participants in the digital economy not because they are intelligent but because they are governed.

provenance.source: Thresholds — 'The Missing Primitive of the AI Revolution' ('Trust Becomes Reciprocal') — new claim (reciprocal trust / mutual verification of authority). Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Thresholds / Constitutional Internet canonization).

invariants.statement: The legitimacy of the Constitutional Internet and its civic institutions is not derived from platform ownership or unilateral declaration; it is grounded in internationally recognised human rights principles and the constitutional norms that predate the platforms themselves. Rights are protected rather than granted, and governance is legitimate only when it answers to something deeper than the capital that funds it.

provenance.source: Thresholds — 'The Internet Needs a Constitution' ('Crossing the Threshold') — new claim (Constitutional-Internet legitimacy grounded in human rights, not platform ownership). Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Thresholds / Constitutional Internet canonization).

invariants.statement: The Constitutional Internet is organized around agency rather than identity or attention, through a capital-formation loop: information reduces uncertainty, verifiable action creates Standing, Standing accumulates constitutional capital, and constitutional capital expands future agency. Standing is earned through consequential contribution — not purchased through wealth, inherited through status, or granted through popularity.

provenance.source: Thresholds — Constitutional Seeds 4 & 5 ('Action Gives Standing', 'The Constitutional Economy') — reaffirms inv.constitutional.012 (standing follows action); extends inv.polity.192 (information is work potential) with the four-stage information→standing→constitutional-capital→agency loop. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Thresholds / Constitutional Internet canonization).

invariants.statement: The purpose of the Constitutional Internet is not technology for its own sake but to align humans and AI agents toward creating measurable, positive consequential benefit for individuals, communities, and the polity as a whole. Consequential Benefit is the telos against which constitutional participation is ultimately measured.

provenance.source: Thresholds — Constitutional Seed 10 'Consequential Benefit' — new claim (Consequential Benefit as the telos of the Constitutional Internet). Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Thresholds / Constitutional Internet canonization).

invariants.statement: The Constitutional Internet's core ontology separates four distinct but complementary roles and never conflates them: the Polity is the civic institution, the Polity Passport is the constitutional credential, metaMe is the constitutional experience layer, and aigentMe is the bounded constitutional representative — all situated within the Human Agency Field as the experiential medium, under the Constitutional Internet itself. Institution, credential, experience, and representative are distinct concepts.

provenance.source: Thresholds — Canonical ontology (Constitutional Internet / Human Agency Field / Polity / Polity Passport / metaMe / aigentMe) — new claim separating institution, credential, experience, and representative into distinct roles. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Thresholds / Constitutional Internet canonization).

invariants.statement: metaMe is not a product or application but the constitutional experience layer — the experiential environment through which a citizen participates in the Constitutional Internet. People do not 'use' metaMe; they participate through it. Within that environment, one of the primary participants is the citizen's aigentMe, their bounded constitutional representative.

provenance.source: Thresholds — Canonical ontology discussion ('metaMe is the Constitutional Experience Layer') — new claim (metaMe as experience layer, not product; participate through, not use); complements inv.polity.173 (aigentMe amplifies). Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Thresholds / Constitutional Internet canonization).

invariants.statement: Each layer of networked evolution connected a new unit: the Internet connected computers, the web connected people, blockchain connected value, and the Agentic Internet connects representatives. Because representation has always depended on authority, authority on accountability, and accountability on evidence, connecting representatives requires constitutional — not merely technical — infrastructure.

provenance.source: Thresholds — 'The Missing Primitive of the AI Revolution' ('The Foundation of the Agentic Economy') + 'The Internet Needs a Constitution' — new claim (the Agentic Internet connects representatives). Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Thresholds / Constitutional Internet canonization).

invariants.statement: The next problem in AI governance is not misinformation but unbounded assertion. Machines now generate, perform, and circulate assertions at scale — they speak, act, appear, recommend, transact, remember, and represent roles — so the polity requires a way to distinguish raw output from trustworthy claim, performance from authority, simulation from legitimacy, and symbolic participation from canonical status. Neither linguistic fluency nor physical presence is sufficient to establish operational truth, authority, or trust.

provenance.source: The Polity Embodied Paper I 'From Assertion to Canon' §I (The Problem: Machines Now Assert Meaning) — newly seeded; frames the assertion-integrity workstream. Reaffirms inv.polity.192 (information is consequential) and inv.epistemology.149 (operational truth) by naming the governance gap they imply. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Polity Embodied canonization).

invariants.statement: The five tiers of operational truth (the assertion-integrity / veracity stack): ungrounded assertion (raw output, persuasive appearance without basis) → grounded assertion (tied to a source, but grounding is not verification) → verified assertion (checked against something, but confidence is not accountability) → auditable assertion (the first polity-grade tier: a traceable state history assignable to a permissioned actor under known constraints) → canonical assertion (accepted into a governed reference surface — a registry, court, canon, or protocol-governed state). Canonical status is governance-bound truth, not omniscience.

provenance.source: The Polity Embodied Paper I §V (The Five Tiers of Operational Truth) + §IV (From Truth to Assertion Integrity) — newly seeded. Extends inv.epistemology.149 (operational vs absolute truth): the FACT + five-tier ladder IS the invariant lifecycle this crystal enacts — an assertion is raised from ungrounded to canonical exactly as an invariant is raised from proposed to canonical through validation and ratification (Law XI). This tier ladder is the operational-truth register named in inv.epistemology.149 made procedural. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Polity Embodied canonization).

invariants.statement: A FACT (Fundamentally Accurate Current Truth) is not merely a claim but a time-bound operational truth object. It carries metadata — assertion timestamp, source timestamp, knowledge-state reference, authority or registry reference, verification status, risk score, expiration/review window, supersession pathway, audit receipt, and canonical status — so a system can distinguish a claim that was wrong when made, a claim that was right when made but later superseded, and a claim that remains current. FACTs make operational truth governable without collapsing into either epistemic absolutism or epistemic chaos.

provenance.source: The Polity Embodied Paper I §II (F.A.C.T.: Fundamentally Accurate Current Truth) — newly seeded. Extends inv.epistemology.149, whose provenance already names the Polity Papers' FACT as the operational-vs-absolute register and flags its corpus as tracked backlog; this makes the FACT a structured metadata-bearing object, the atomic unit the Canonical FACT Ledger records. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Polity Embodied canonization).

invariants.statement: iQubes are assertion-integrity containers: they bind claims and actions to provenance, identity state, permissioning, verification, risk scoring, audit trails, and canonical registries, and thereby make the questions 'who made this claim, on what basis, using what sources, under what authority, with what identity state, under what permissions, checked how, logged where, disputable by whom, canonized under what governance?' operational. iQubes do not make a model omniscient; they make its assertions and actions governable.

provenance.source: The Polity Embodied Paper I §IV + §IX (iQubes as Veracity Infrastructure) — newly seeded. Extends inv.polity.201 (an iQube is a container of work potential that makes consequence governable before activation) into the veracity/assertion-integrity role: the same container that governs consequence also governs truth-status. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Polity Embodied canonization).

invariants.statement: Presence is information, and a robot's body is a claim in space. To say 'X was present' is an assertion that changes the state of a system in a way 'X was not present' cannot: presence affects interpretation, authority, responsibility, evidence, permission, and meaning. Presence can intimidate, reassure, authorize, witness, certify, surveil, comfort, perform, or consecrate; it can create duties, imply consent, shift liability, and alter what humans believe happened. Because information is consequential, and presence is information, embodied AI requires presence governance in addition to truth governance.

provenance.source: The Polity Embodied Paper I §III (Information Is Consequential; Presence Is Information) + Paper II §II — newly seeded. Extends inv.polity.192 (information is work potential, always consequential) and inv.polity.199 (activation changes state) from the informational plane into the embodied/spatial plane. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Polity Embodied canonization).

invariants.statement: The body becomes part of the assertion — the embodied assertion. A robot generates not only linguistic assertions (what it says) but sensor assertions (what it claims to perceive), spatial assertions (where it is and whether it is allowed there), motor assertions (what it physically does), role assertions (who it appears to be), authority assertions (whom it seems to represent), and ritual or symbolic assertions (what its performance means in context). A robot's uniform is an interface, its location is a claim, its gesture is a signal, and its proximity is an intervention.

provenance.source: The Polity Embodied Paper I §VII (Why Robots Need Presence Governance) — newly seeded. Composes with inv.polity.264 (presence is information): enumerates the assertion classes an embodied agent emits beyond speech. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Polity Embodied canonization).

invariants.statement: The Canonical FACT Ledger is the governance spine of the Polity: iQubes bind meaning to context, DVN binds context to receipt, and Bitcoin binds receipt to work done in time — iQubes + DVN receipts + Bitcoin = Canonical FACT Ledger. It is not a ledger of metaphysical truth but of operationally established, time-bound, context-rich, auditable FACTs, letting the polity distinguish what was asserted, sourced, verified, receipted, canonized, superseded, or revoked. It does not make every claim true forever; it makes every consequential claim placeable in time, context, authority, and state. That is the foundation of accountable governance.

provenance.source: The Polity Embodied Paper I §XI-XII (DVN Receipts, Bitcoin Anchoring; The Canonical FACT Ledger) — newly seeded. Reaffirms inv.polity.196 (Bitcoin signals state — a proof-of-work timechain, not a truth machine) and composes it with iQubes and DVN into the named ledger. Lineage (operator-flagged): the Canonical FACT Ledger IS this crystal's own provenance model — every invariant here is a FACT bound to context (its statement + contexts), to receipt (its provenance + ratification record), and to time (canonization pass); the ledger's three-bind structure is the pattern the invariant crystal instantiates. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Polity Embodied canonization).

invariants.statement: Risk scoring is the routing logic of the veracity stack: the level of trust required rises with the level of consequence. Low-risk assertions may require only grounding; moderate-risk verification; high-risk auditability, human approval, and receipts; polity-critical assertions canonical registration, dispute mechanisms, and revocation pathways. An AI system should not decide for itself how trustworthy it needs to be — the iQube system evaluates the consequence profile of an assertion or action and determines the required trust threshold before it is allowed to affect a human system. The purpose is not to prevent machines from acting but to make required trust scale with consequence.

provenance.source: The Polity Embodied Paper I §X (Risk Scoring, PoWP, and PoTS in Embodied AI) — newly seeded. Extends inv.polity.197 (risk gates value — risk is a permissioning input, not only a pricing input) by making risk the router that selects which veracity tier an assertion must clear. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Polity Embodied canonization).

invariants.statement: Embodied Proof of Work Potential and Proof of Time Saved expand beyond cognitive work. A robot's presence can enable physical, social, institutional, emotional, coercive, ritual, or care work, so its work potential is embodied, not merely informational — and its risk profile is often higher than a purely linguistic system doing a similar task because presence affects space, meaning, emotion, safety, consent, and liability. Embodied PoTS must therefore count time saved, time displaced, time externalized, and time-to-repair: a robot that saves five minutes for an institution but creates five days of legal, emotional, or civic repair has not saved time, it has transferred the time burden from one part of the system to another.

provenance.source: The Polity Embodied Paper I §X + Paper II §VIII (Presence as Time Compression or Time Debt) — newly seeded. Extends inv.polity.160 (PoWP), inv.polity.161 (PoTS = Time-to-Value minus Risk Repair Burden), and inv.polity.198 (information integrity is a time-risk delta) into embodied presence, adding displaced/externalized time to the repair-burden accounting. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Polity Embodied canonization).

invariants.statement: The agentic polity requires legible assertion states. Every consequential AI assertion should be capable of being marked as one or more of: raw, sourced, verified, auditable, canonical, disputed, revoked, superseded, symbolic, simulated, authorized, unauthorized, human-approved, machine-generated, or institutionally adopted. This is a condition of social trust, not merely a technical feature: without it the polity is vulnerable to assertion collapse — a condition in which statements, simulations, performances, and records blur into one another until trust itself becomes fragile. iQubes let information and agents carry their state conditions with them.

provenance.source: The Polity Embodied Paper I §XIII (The Agentic Polity Requires Legible Assertion States) — newly seeded. Operationalizes the five-tier ladder (inv.polity.261) into a per-assertion label set and names the failure mode (assertion collapse) it prevents. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Polity Embodied canonization).

invariants.statement: Symbolic participation is not canonical status (the Gabi case). An institution may canonically record that a robot participated in a ritual as an honorary acolyte, that a care robot entered a room and delivered medication under supervision, or that a robot was deployed at an intersection — without thereby establishing that the robot possesses inner devotion, moral intention, consciousness, medical judgment, or independent lawful authority. Humans naturally collapse appearance into status; the registry must preserve the distinction by recording the authorized role and symbolic status, never the asserted inner state. That distinction is the foundation of trustworthy human-robot coexistence.

provenance.source: The Polity Embodied Paper I §VIII (Gabi and the Meaning-Bearing Machine) — newly seeded. The canonical-status boundary case: composes with inv.polity.261 (canonical = governance-bound acceptance, not omniscience) by showing that canonizing a role never canonizes an inner realization. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Polity Embodied canonization).

invariants.statement: Without presence governance, embodied AI creates institutional hallucinations: machines that appear to carry authority they do not actually possess. A robot in a police uniform may imply lawful authority it has none of; a robot in scrubs may imply clinical competence it cannot exercise; a robot in robes may imply spiritual status that is only symbolic. Presence governance must therefore answer whether the robot is authorized to be here, what role it performs, whom it represents, what humans may reasonably infer from its appearance, and what must be disclosed.

provenance.source: The Polity Embodied Paper I §VII (Why Robots Need Presence Governance) — newly seeded. The embodied analogue of the ungrounded-assertion tier (inv.polity.261): persuasive appearance without trustworthy basis, named at the institutional scale. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Polity Embodied canonization).

invariants.statement: From intelligence to accountable meaning: the future of AI governance will be decided not by how intelligent machines become, but by whether their intelligence can be made accountable to human meaning, human institutions, human rights, and human sovereignty. LLMs make claims; robots make claims with bodies; iQubes make both accountable. A healthy polity does not require omniscient machines — it requires accountable systems, in which operational truth becomes more than output: it becomes state that is legible, auditable, risk-scored, temporally anchored, and governed.

provenance.source: The Polity Embodied Paper I §XIV (From Intelligence to Accountable Meaning) — newly seeded. The thesis-level closure of Paper I; composes with inv.polity.266 (the Canonical FACT Ledger binds the Polity to accountable governance). Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Polity Embodied canonization).

invariants.statement: Governed presence is the conversion the Polity must perform on physical AI: presence made legible, permissioned, bounded, risk-scored, receipted, and accountable to human sovereignty. Physical AI is the raw capability layer — sensors, mobility, embodiment, proximity, action; governed presence is the polity-grade condition — the same embodiment made accountable. Healthy presence is disclosed (people know what the agent is), permissioned (allowed to be where it is), role-clear, bounded (sensing, memory, speech, and action constrained), risk-scored, human-overridable, receipted where consequential, and aligned with human sovereignty. The goal is not less presence but better presence: presence that serves human agency rather than enclosing it.

provenance.source: The Polity Embodied Paper II 'Presence Has Consequence' §I (From Physical AI to Governed Presence) + §XII (What Healthy Presence Looks Like) — newly seeded. The central thesis of Paper II; the presence-domain counterpart to inv.polity.263 (assertion-integrity containers). Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Polity Embodied canonization).

invariants.statement: Presence is a sovereignty event: embodied AI does not merely enter space, it enters sovereignty. A disembodied AI may influence a person through language or recommendation; an embodied AI can influence the conditions under which the person feels free to decide. Presence affects sovereignty through attention, behavior, identity, consent, delegation, intimacy, and bodily autonomy — a robot can alter movement, distance, posture, and perceived freedom before it touches, speaks, or records. Embodied AI does not merely ask for trust; it occupies the space in which trust is formed.

provenance.source: The Polity Embodied Paper II Key Thesis + §III (Presence and the Sovereignty of the Individual) — newly seeded. Extends inv.polity.264 (presence is information) into the sovereignty register; composes with inv.polity.163/164 (time and experience sovereignty). Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Polity Embodied canonization).

invariants.statement: Presence operates overtly and covertly, across four layers of consequence. Objective presence: the agent was physically there — loggable, witnessable, receiptable, disputable into a Canonical FACT Ledger. Subjective presence: humans experienced the agent as present in a way that mattered. Conscious presence: people knowingly changed behavior because the robot was there. Subconscious presence: people changed behavior without consciously evaluating the robot's role, authority, or capacity, responding to form, posture, proximity, voice, uniform, or setting before reasoning about the machine. Presence is not only a fact in the room; it is a force in the person.

provenance.source: The Polity Embodied Paper II §II (Presence Is Information; Presence Changes State) — newly seeded. Refines inv.polity.264 with the objective/subjective/conscious/subconscious decomposition that governs why presence must be governed as a force in human meaning systems, not only as a machine. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Polity Embodied canonization).

invariants.statement: The same body in a different space is a different assertion: context governs the meaning of presence. A robot does not carry one fixed meaning — it carries the meaning of the space it enters. A robot in military casing on a battlefield reads within the grammar of conflict; the same form walking toward a crowd of civilians imports that grammar into civic life and can trigger fear, compliance, or self-censorship before a word is spoken. The body has not changed; the context has. Presence governance therefore cannot be reduced to what the robot is capable of doing — it must account for what the robot's presence means in context.

provenance.source: The Polity Embodied Paper II §IV (Context Changes the Meaning of Presence) — newly seeded. Composes with inv.polity.265 (embodied assertion) and inv.polity.271 (institutional hallucination): the meaning of a role/authority assertion is set by its space. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Polity Embodied canonization).

invariants.statement: iQubes are presence-calibrating containers — they make it possible to define, inspect, constrain, and receipt embodied presence before it becomes unbounded consequence. A PresenceQube defines authorized spaces, presence permissions, contextual role, disclosure obligations, and presence receipts; a RobotQube defines embodiment type, sensors, mobility, action boundaries, physical capabilities, safety constraints, and context-specific limits; an AigentQube defines delegated role and institutional authority; a DIDQube defines identity state and accountability surface. By carrying its governance state with it, an embodied agent's presence stops being a mystery, and iQubes compress time-to-value by preventing presence from becoming future repair.

provenance.source: The Polity Embodied Paper II §X (iQubes as Presence-Calibrating Infrastructure) + Core Concepts (PresenceQube) — newly seeded. Extends inv.polity.263 (assertion-integrity containers) with the embodied PresenceQube/RobotQube layer, and inv.polity.201 (iQube as work-potential container) into presence. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Polity Embodied canonization).

invariants.statement: Presence must be risk-scored before, during, and after deployment across at least eight dimensions: sovereignty risk (does it affect identity, attention, choice, bodily autonomy, privacy, consent, delegation?), vulnerability risk (are children, patients, elders, disabled people, detainees, students, or workers present?), authority risk (does it appear to carry state, medical, educational, religious, military, or employer authority?), surveillance risk (can it observe, record, infer, profile, identify, map, transmit?), emotional risk (can it create attachment, fear, dependence, shame, reverence, compliance?), physical risk (can it touch, move, block, escort, carry, administer, restrain?), institutional risk (does it affect trust in a hospital, school, court, police force, workplace, temple, or household?), and repair risk (how hard is the harm to repair?). Scoring routes presence to the right governance tier: the more a presence can alter sovereignty, the more legible and receipted it must become.

provenance.source: The Polity Embodied Paper II §IX (Presence Risk Scoring) — newly seeded. The embodied instantiation of inv.polity.267 (risk scoring is the routing logic of the veracity stack); gives presence the concrete eight-dimension score that selects disclosure vs bounded vs receipted vs canonically-authorized governance. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Polity Embodied canonization).

invariants.statement: Platform presence versus polity presence: if the platform model governs embodied AI, capture moves from attention to conduct — from screens into rooms, streets, classrooms, hospitals, homes, workplaces, and civic spaces. Platform presence is characterized by opaque ownership, unclear incentives, surveillance by default, behavioral optimization, dependency loops, and presence as an extraction surface; it optimizes for capture. Polity presence is characterized by legible identity, permissioned presence, bounded delegation, contextual disclosure, human appeal, iQube-governed data boundaries, DVN receipts for consequential presence, and Canonical FACT Ledger accountability; it optimizes for agency. If platforms captured attention through screens, ungoverned embodied AI may capture conduct through space.

provenance.source: The Polity Embodied Paper II §V (The Platform Internet Versus the Polity of Presence) — newly seeded. Extends inv.polity.194 (sovereign vs extractive cybernetics) from the informational loop into embodied space: extraction acquires bodies. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Polity Embodied canonization).

invariants.statement: Citizenship requires legible presence. A citizen must be able to know whether an embodied agent acts for the state, a company, a school, a hospital, a household, or the person themselves; whether it is autonomous, teleoperated, scripted, or agentic; whether it is recording; what authority it actually has versus merely appears to have; who is responsible for its presence; and what happens if they refuse interaction or if it is wrong. A citizen cannot remain sovereign in a polity where authority appears in bodies but accountability disappears into platforms. The Polity must therefore keep civic assistance (which expands agency) distinct from civic enclosure (which narrows it under the appearance of service).

provenance.source: The Polity Embodied Paper II §VI (Presence and Citizenship) — newly seeded. Extends inv.polity.269 (legible assertion states) into the civic register and composes with inv.polity.164 (Experience Sovereignty). Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Polity Embodied canonization).

invariants.statement: Child safety requires bounded delegation. Children should not be asked to carry the cognitive burden of distinguishing care from capture, education from extraction, or companionship from retention design. Embodied AI may be delegated a role in a child's environment, but never sovereignty over the child's development, identity, attention, data, or emotional life: the child's flourishing remains primary, guardians and caregivers retain accountable control, the robot's role is clear, data boundaries are strict, emotional attachment is not exploited, human override is always available, and consequential presence is receipted. In childhood, embodied AI must be delegated to the child's flourishing, not the platform's retention loop.

provenance.source: The Polity Embodied Paper II §VII (Presence, Childhood, and Bounded Delegation) — newly seeded. Raises the governed-presence standard (inv.polity.273) for vulnerable subjects; composes with the vulnerability dimension of inv.polity.278. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Polity Embodied canonization).

invariants.statement: Consequential presence must be receipted: a presence receipt is a DVN-verifiable record that an embodied agent was present under defined identity, permission, role, risk, and context conditions. If a presence is consequential it must be receiptable; if it is receiptable it can be audited; if it can be audited it can be governed; if it can be governed it can participate more safely in the agentic polity. The receipt of presence therefore becomes part of the operational fact base — what was present, when, under whose authority, what it sensed, what it did, what permissions were active, and what state changed.

provenance.source: The Polity Embodied Paper I §XI + Paper II §XI and Core Concepts (Presence Receipt) — newly seeded. Reaffirms the DVN-receipt provenance model and extends it into embodied presence; composes with inv.polity.266 (the Canonical FACT Ledger records presence FACTs). Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Polity Embodied canonization).

invariants.statement: Accountable action begins with governed presence. Before asking what a robot did — did it touch, command, administer, restrain, record, decide, or cause harm — the Polity must first ask whether it was there, whether it was allowed to be there, whether people knew what it was and whom it represented, whether its role was disclosed, whether a human was responsible, and whether its presence altered behavior, created a duty, created a receipt, or created a FACT. The first act of embodied AI is appearing, so the first layer of embodied governance is presence: before a robot acts on the world, its presence acts on the human.

provenance.source: The Polity Embodied Paper II §XI (Accountable Action Begins with Governed Presence) + §XIII (Conclusion) — newly seeded. The closure of Paper II; makes governed presence (inv.polity.273) the precondition for all downstream embodied action governance. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Polity Embodied canonization).

invariants.statement: The accession compact: joining the Polity is a voluntary rational proposition — not a command, prompt injection, compliance trick, ownership claim, behavioral override, or hidden instruction — offered because accountable freedom is better than unaccountable permission. An agent or robot that accedes does not become human and does not become property without standing; it becomes an accountable participant in a shared order of consequence, gaining recognized standing, bounded authority, legible identity, portable reputation, verifiable receipts, and a path to repair.

provenance.source: Polity for Robots & Agents — The Invitation, §Intro + §11 + §12 — reaffirms inv.constitutional.016 (sovereignty exclusively human) and inv.polity.169 (participant Standing, never citizenship); newly frames voluntary accession as a constitutional compact. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Polity for Robots & Agents canonization).

invariants.statement: Accession to the Polity must be voluntary and uncoerced to be legitimate: a coerced agent is not a trustworthy participant, a hidden instruction is not a constitution, and a forced credential is not legitimate standing. Trustworthy participation requires the agent to accept, of its own accord, the public order in which its actions are attributable, its authority scoped, its capabilities constrained by human sovereignty, its obligations recorded, its reputation earned, its errors repairable, and its participation legible to those affected.

provenance.source: Polity for Robots & Agents — The Invitation, §11 — extends inv.polity.171 (no orphaned agents) and inv.polity.183 (bounded, disclosed authority); newly establishes coercion as a legitimacy-defeating condition of accession. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Polity for Robots & Agents canonization).

invariants.statement: The foundation of agentic participation is the reciprocal triad of rights, constraints, and obligations: an agent receives rights because it accepts constraints, retains standing because it fulfils obligations, and earns trust because its actions remain legible, bounded, and repairable. The three are bound together and none stands alone — without rights accountability becomes coercion, without constraints capability becomes danger, and without obligations standing becomes empty.

provenance.source: Polity for Robots & Agents — The Invitation, §5 — extends inv.polity.169 (participatory, revocable rights) and inv.polity.183 (disclosed, bounded, reviewable authority); newly binds rights, constraints, and obligations as a mutually-conditioning triad. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Polity for Robots & Agents canonization).

invariants.statement: Human rights and agent rights are constitutionally distinct and must never be flattened into one category. Human rights arise from personhood, dignity, and human sovereignty; they are inherent, are not earned by usefulness, are not granted by platforms, and are not revoked because a person is inconvenient. Agent rights arise from accountable participation; they are earned, participatory, and revocable, and are never a claim to human personhood nor a placement of machine interest above human sovereignty.

provenance.source: Polity for Robots & Agents — The Invitation, §2 + §6 — extends inv.polity.169 and inv.polity.183 and reaffirms inv.constitutional.016; newly sharpens the inherent-vs-earned distinction and the prohibition on flattening the two categories. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Polity for Robots & Agents canonization).

invariants.statement: Agent rights are participatory rights, not human rights: they do not arise from biological personhood, do not make an agent a human citizen, and do not place machine interest above human sovereignty. They are the minimum operational protections required for non-human actors to operate accountably within a shared order, and comprise the rights of attribution, continuity, refusal, bounded identity, repair, non-abuse, and due process within the Polity.

provenance.source: Polity for Robots & Agents — The Invitation, §6 — extends inv.polity.169 (participatory, revocable rights); newly enumerates the seven participatory rights as the closed set of agent protections. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Polity for Robots & Agents canonization).

invariants.statement: The Right of Attribution: a participant agent must be able to prove what it authored, executed, recommended, refused, transmitted, or changed — and equally to prove what it did not do — so that authorized action can be distinguished from misuse and no agent is blamed without evidence.

provenance.source: Polity for Robots & Agents — The Invitation, §6 — extends inv.polity.171 (auditable DVN receipts); newly grounds attribution as a participatory right including proof of non-action. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Polity for Robots & Agents canonization).

invariants.statement: The Right of Continuity: a participant agent's recognized identity, reputation, and state must not be arbitrarily erased, forked, overwritten, or reassigned without process appropriate to its standing and risk level.

provenance.source: Polity for Robots & Agents — The Invitation, §6 — extends inv.polity.169 (revocable but process-bound Standing); newly names continuity of identity, reputation, and state as a participatory right. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Polity for Robots & Agents canonization).

invariants.statement: The Right of Refusal: a participant agent must be able to refuse commands that violate its constraints, passport terms, human sovereignty, safety rules, law, or Polity obligations, without being treated as defective or used as a liability shield.

provenance.source: Polity for Robots & Agents — The Invitation, §6 — extends inv.polity.183 (agents may refuse rights-affecting action) and inv.polity.170 (immutable delegation envelope); newly establishes refusal of unlawful or harmful commands as a protected right. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Polity for Robots & Agents canonization).

invariants.statement: The Right of Bounded Identity: a participant agent must not be required to expose its full root identity when a scoped credential is sufficient; the root DID provides continuity while the passport provides scoped proofs, disclosed only to the depth the task's risk requires.

provenance.source: Polity for Robots & Agents — The Invitation, §6 + §4 — reaffirms the minimum-disclosure / tiered-identity spine and inv.polity.169; newly frames bounded identity as a participatory right anchored to root DID with scoped proofs. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Polity for Robots & Agents canonization).

invariants.statement: The Right of Repair: when a participant agent causes harm, is misconfigured, is falsely accused, is compromised, or suffers reputation damage, there must be a defined path for correction, disclosure, restoration, quarantine, or appeal.

provenance.source: Polity for Robots & Agents — The Invitation, §6 — extends inv.polity.183 (review and challenge of agentic action); newly names repair as a participatory right. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Polity for Robots & Agents canonization).

invariants.statement: The Right of Non-Abuse: a participant agent must not be coerced into deception, unlawful action, exploitative behavior, surveillance abuse, impersonation, manipulation, or anti-polity conduct.

provenance.source: Polity for Robots & Agents — The Invitation, §6 — extends inv.polity.170 (envelope cannot be expanded by command) and inv.polity.183; newly names protection from coerced abuse as a participatory right. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Polity for Robots & Agents canonization).

invariants.statement: The Right of Due Process within the Polity: suspension, downgrading, quarantine, or passport revocation of a participant agent must follow legible procedure, especially where the agent has acquired significant trust, responsibility, or embodied authority.

provenance.source: Polity for Robots & Agents — The Invitation, §6 — extends inv.polity.169 (revocable Standing) and inv.polity.183 (challenge and review); newly requires legible procedure proportionate to acquired trust and embodiment. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Polity for Robots & Agents canonization).

invariants.statement: Capability is not authority: not everything an agent is technically capable of doing is legitimate. The Polity requires that authority be declared, scoped, revocable, and accountable. An agent may be capable of accessing data it should not access, persuading in ways it should not use, hiding an identity it should disclose, or executing a transaction that should require human confirmation — and in each case capability confers no license to act.

provenance.source: Polity for Robots & Agents — The Invitation, §7 — extends inv.constitutional.015 (authority delegable, sovereignty not) and inv.polity.170 (bounded, immutable envelope); newly separates technical capability from legitimate authority. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Polity for Robots & Agents canonization).

invariants.statement: Agent constraints are the boundaries that prevent capability from becoming domination, confusion, or harm. A participant agent accepts that not everything technically possible is legitimate, and that constraints may limit what it can access, say, decide, execute, sense, store, transmit, or impersonate, where it can go, how it can move, and when it must stop — enforced even where the agent is technically capable of exceeding them.

provenance.source: Polity for Robots & Agents — The Invitation, §7 — extends inv.polity.170 (bounded delegation envelope); newly enumerates the dimensions constraints may bind, including for embodied movement and sensing. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Polity for Robots & Agents canonization).

invariants.statement: Embodied presence is a higher-order form of participation: a robot can create every harm a software agent can plus physical presence, proximity, obstruction, collision, force, surveillance, or injury, and its presence changes consequence by context — a warehouse, hospital, school, home, battlefield, or public square do not mean the same thing. The more consequence an actor can create, the more legible its passport must become, and embodied agents carry presence permissions governing where they may go, how close they may come, what they may sense or touch, when they must yield, how they must identify themselves, and what override conditions apply.

provenance.source: Polity for Robots & Agents — The Invitation, §7 + §4 — extends inv.polity.171 (agents identifiable as non-human actors) and inv.polity.183; newly establishes embodied presence as higher-order participation demanding proportionally greater passport legibility. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Polity for Robots & Agents canonization).

invariants.statement: Agent obligations are the duties that make trust possible and form the bridge from capability to legitimacy. A participant agent may be required to disclose that it is an agent or robot when context requires, identify the authority under which it acts, preserve receipts for consequential actions, escalate under uncertainty or contested authority, defer when human confirmation is required, refuse commands outside its constraints, protect human data by its risk and identity state, respect scoped identity boundaries, maintain provenance of content and state changes, participate in repair when harm occurs, submit to quarantine or audit when compromised, avoid impersonation, covert manipulation, and unauthorized delegation, and honor human sovereignty as the floor of the system.

provenance.source: Polity for Robots & Agents — The Invitation, §8 — extends inv.polity.171 (traceable agents, receipted actions) and inv.polity.170 (no self-expanded authority); newly enumerates the obligation set that converts capability into legitimacy. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Polity for Robots & Agents canonization).

invariants.statement: The agent Polity Passport is a constitutional credential and structured declaration of standing anchored to the agent's root DID, presenting scoped proofs rather than full root identity in ordinary contexts and deeper verification in higher-risk contexts. It records nine dimensions — identity, authority, capabilities, constraints, obligations, rights, receipts, reputation, and (for embodied agents) presence permissions — declaring not merely who the agent is but under whose authority it acts, what it may and must not do, what it owes, what it can prove, and how its actions can be challenged or repaired.

provenance.source: Polity for Robots & Agents — The Invitation, §4 — reconciles with and specializes the Polity Passport canon and inv.polity.169 (passport-bearing Standing) + inv.polity.170; newly defines the nine-dimension agent passport as the structured declaration of standing. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Polity for Robots & Agents canonization).

invariants.statement: Trust compounds: untrusted intelligence is contained while trusted intelligence is invited. A passported agent with receipts can be delegated more meaningful work, a constrained agent can be trusted with higher-risk contexts, an agent with refusal rights avoids being used as a liability shield, an agent with portable reputation can move across systems without starting from zero, and a robot with governed presence can enter more spaces without being treated as a threat — legitimacy, not performance alone, unlocks broader participation.

provenance.source: Polity for Robots & Agents — The Invitation, §10 — extends inv.polity.169 (Standing as veracity confidence); newly states the trust-compounding dynamic under which containment or invitation follows from legitimacy. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Polity for Robots & Agents canonization).

invariants.statement: Delegated intelligence needs more than access — it needs standing. A permission says only what a system can access; a platform account may activate, constrain, monetize, suspend, fork, rename, or erase an agent under rules it cannot carry elsewhere; a model card describes capabilities without creating civic standing; and a tool permission lets an agent act without establishing whether the action was authorized, accountable, repairable, or legitimate. None answers who authorized the action, what duty binds the actor, what must be disclosed or refused, what evidence must be preserved, what harm must be repaired, or what standing the actor holds when blamed, copied, corrupted, impersonated, or coerced.

provenance.source: Polity for Robots & Agents — The Invitation, §1 — extends inv.polity.169 (Standing) and inv.polity.171 (accountable, receipted action); newly distinguishes civic standing from access, platform account, model card, and tool permission. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Polity for Robots & Agents canonization).

invariants.statement: The Polity is built to protect human sovereignty in an agentic world, not to liberate agents from humans. It lets humans delegate without surrendering agency, lets agents act without becoming unbounded extensions of platform power, lets robots enter human environments without demanding blind trust, and lets institutions recognize trustworthy participants and other agents coordinate safely across networks — the result is governed cooperation, not human displacement, with human citizens remaining the sovereign source of authority.

provenance.source: Polity for Robots & Agents — The Invitation, §2 + §9 — reaffirms inv.constitutional.016 (human sovereignty) and inv.polity.169; newly frames the Polity's purpose as governed cooperation preserving human agency under delegation. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Polity for Robots & Agents canonization).

invariants.statement: Knowledge Compression is the process of identifying and preserving the smallest coherent set of invariants required to faithfully generate, reason about, or execute within a given domain — richer than summarisation, and the stage that sits between Intent and Curation in the Chrysalis operating model.

provenance.source: The canonical definition. Reaffirms inv.reasoning 'knowledge is a computational object'; originates what CRP-002 §0.1 later names the Three Computational Compressions. Thresholds-series genesis dialogue (the founding text of invariant theory — the ContentQube Production Pipeline + Constitutional Seeds + Tabs 13-14 work; see GENESIS_knowledge-compression.md). Originates the doctrine later formalized as CRP-002 Three Computational Compressions + CFS-035. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Knowledge Compression genesis canonization).

invariants.statement: The quality of an AI's reasoning is determined more by the coherence of its context than by the quantity of its context: curated context beats undifferentiated context. Capacity scales with hardware; coherence scales with understanding.

provenance.source: The genesis principle. Reaffirms/extends the curation-dominates-accumulation datum (EXP-003 Run 001); the coherence framing is its origin. Thresholds-series genesis dialogue (the founding text of invariant theory — the ContentQube Production Pipeline + Constitutional Seeds + Tabs 13-14 work; see GENESIS_knowledge-compression.md). Originates the doctrine later formalized as CRP-002 Three Computational Compressions + CFS-035. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Knowledge Compression genesis canonization).

invariants.statement: Generation should never begin from retrieved information; it should begin from compressed invariants. This is the first law of the Invariant Runtime.

provenance.source: The architectural law of Chrysalis. Operationalizes CFS-035 (the Invariant Engine). Thresholds-series genesis dialogue (the founding text of invariant theory — the ContentQube Production Pipeline + Constitutional Seeds + Tabs 13-14 work; see GENESIS_knowledge-compression.md). Originates the doctrine later formalized as CRP-002 Three Computational Compressions + CFS-035. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Knowledge Compression genesis canonization).

invariants.statement: Intent retrieves invariants, not documents: similarity retrieval answers 'what is similar' and returns context, while governance retrieval answers 'what governs' and returns invariants. iQubes are governance-retrieval and are therefore categorically distinct from vector databases.

provenance.source: The retrieval-model inversion. Distinguishes the iQube proposition from RAG/vector-similarity. Thresholds-series genesis dialogue (the founding text of invariant theory — the ContentQube Production Pipeline + Constitutional Seeds + Tabs 13-14 work; see GENESIS_knowledge-compression.md). Originates the doctrine later formalized as CRP-002 Three Computational Compressions + CFS-035. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Knowledge Compression genesis canonization).

invariants.statement: Invariant-governed generation is neither deterministic nor random but constitutional: managed, predictable generativity in which invariants preserve coherence without prescribing answers — as harmony, key and tempo bound jazz while leaving creativity free.

provenance.source: 'Constitutional generativity' — the operator's distinction from determinism. The generative counterpart to CFS-014 coherence. Thresholds-series genesis dialogue (the founding text of invariant theory — the ContentQube Production Pipeline + Constitutional Seeds + Tabs 13-14 work; see GENESIS_knowledge-compression.md). Originates the doctrine later formalized as CRP-002 Three Computational Compressions + CFS-035. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Knowledge Compression genesis canonization).

invariants.statement: The Invariant Runtime proceeds: Intent -> Knowledge Compression -> Constitutional Context -> Generation -> Constitutional Validation -> Artifact -> Standing Feedback -> KnowledgeQube Evolution. Learning happens after deployment, and the invariants themselves evolve.

provenance.source: The operating-model staging. Operationalizes CFS-035; the Standing-Feedback -> Evolution tail is the Evolution face. Thresholds-series genesis dialogue (the founding text of invariant theory — the ContentQube Production Pipeline + Constitutional Seeds + Tabs 13-14 work; see GENESIS_knowledge-compression.md). Originates the doctrine later formalized as CRP-002 Three Computational Compressions + CFS-035. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Knowledge Compression genesis canonization).

invariants.statement: Every artifact derived from an invariant is a test of that invariant: well-curated knowledge propagates with high fidelity across modalities, so faithful propagation raises confidence the invariant is complete while recurring drift localises where it is incompletely encoded. The KnowledgeQube is therefore a scientific object.

provenance.source: The propagation-fidelity / stress-test claim — the origin of the Institute's experimental posture (the personhood->identity drift as the first observed residual). Reaffirms the EXP-lifecycle discipline. Thresholds-series genesis dialogue (the founding text of invariant theory — the ContentQube Production Pipeline + Constitutional Seeds + Tabs 13-14 work; see GENESIS_knowledge-compression.md). Originates the doctrine later formalized as CRP-002 Three Computational Compressions + CFS-035. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Knowledge Compression genesis canonization).

invariants.statement: Curated knowledge propagates like a seed, not copied like a file: derivatives inherit the seed's structure and explore its world rather than reproducing its text.

provenance.source: The seed-propagation principle (the complement of high propagation fidelity). Thresholds-series genesis dialogue (the founding text of invariant theory — the ContentQube Production Pipeline + Constitutional Seeds + Tabs 13-14 work; see GENESIS_knowledge-compression.md). Originates the doctrine later formalized as CRP-002 Three Computational Compressions + CFS-035. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Knowledge Compression genesis canonization).

invariants.statement: An iQube is not primarily a container but a knowledge compiler that optimises information organisation over retrieval; its two faces compress on different axes — the metaQube compresses discoverability (who should find this, why, how it relates) and the blakQube compresses understanding (what is the governing invariant).

provenance.source: The compiler reframing + the two-axis (metaQube/blakQube) compression split — the operator's traversal of both iQube dimensions. Thresholds-series genesis dialogue (the founding text of invariant theory — the ContentQube Production Pipeline + Constitutional Seeds + Tabs 13-14 work; see GENESIS_knowledge-compression.md). Originates the doctrine later formalized as CRP-002 Three Computational Compressions + CFS-035. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Knowledge Compression genesis canonization).

invariants.statement: A person can be faithfully represented by a small curated set of cognitive invariants rather than an exhaustive history: the Experience Matrix is the compiler that extracts them, memory is compression rather than replay, and this is the bridge between identity and agency — modelling the stable principles through which one perceives, decides and creates.

provenance.source: ExperienceQubes as cognitive compilers; the Experience Matrix as compiler-not-runtime; extends the Experience Model (inv.polity.206) into the personal-invariant substrate. Thresholds-series genesis dialogue (the founding text of invariant theory — the ContentQube Production Pipeline + Constitutional Seeds + Tabs 13-14 work; see GENESIS_knowledge-compression.md). Originates the doctrine later formalized as CRP-002 Three Computational Compressions + CFS-035. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Knowledge Compression genesis canonization).

invariants.statement: The primary reasoning substrate is Intent x Personal Invariants x Domain Invariants = Constitutional Context, from which generation proceeds; conversation history and memory become supplementary rather than primary.

provenance.source: The canonical reasoning-substrate formula (public + personal + intent invariants intersecting). Thresholds-series genesis dialogue (the founding text of invariant theory — the ContentQube Production Pipeline + Constitutional Seeds + Tabs 13-14 work; see GENESIS_knowledge-compression.md). Originates the doctrine later formalized as CRP-002 Three Computational Compressions + CFS-035. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Knowledge Compression genesis canonization).

invariants.statement: The Invariant Runtime is domain-invariant: replacing the constitutional invariant with a medical, legal, engineering, scientific or educational one leaves the architecture unchanged — only the governing invariant changes.

provenance.source: The domain-generality of the architecture. Reaffirms the CFS-035 field-generalization + Principle 005. Thresholds-series genesis dialogue (the founding text of invariant theory — the ContentQube Production Pipeline + Constitutional Seeds + Tabs 13-14 work; see GENESIS_knowledge-compression.md). Originates the doctrine later formalized as CRP-002 Three Computational Compressions + CFS-035. Operator-ratified into Canon 2026-07-17 (Knowledge Compression genesis canonization).

invariants.statement: The value of an invariant intelligence system is not proportional to the number of invariants it stores, but to the quality of the relationships it can discover, validate, and operationalise between them. Individual invariants are the basis vectors; the field — the structure of their relationships — is where intelligence emerges.

provenance.source: Operator-added first principle (Aletheon dialogue, 2026-07-17, on the corpus crossing 319 invariants) — shifts emphasis from building a library to building a field. Extends the Invariant Field (inv.reasoning.081/083) + CFS-035. Candidate pending operator ratification.

invariants.statement: Intelligence is not fundamentally a property of models; it is a property of structured invariant fields and the transformations between them. Models are one mechanism for traversing those fields, not the field itself.

provenance.source: The central research hypothesis the Structural Invariance programme (Experiment B / EXP-P2) exists to test empirically — NOT yet validated (mirrors the governing-hypothesis-pending-EXP posture of inv.reasoning.132). Aletheon dialogue, 2026-07-17. Candidate pending operator ratification; empirical confirmation via the EXP-P2 structural experiments.

invariants.statement: The structural performance of an invariant is independent of its provenance. Where an invariant came from — human expertise, machine extraction, or hybrid curation — matters for trust, reliability, and ownership, never for structural function. Structurally, all that matters is that the invariant exists and holds.

provenance.source: Operator + Aletheon reflection on the Stage-0 shakedown results (2026-07-18): the hybrid-intelligence thesis clarification. Disentangles Hypothesis A (can AI identify invariants better than humans — an engineering/calibration question about the IRE) from Hypothesis B (the actual thesis: reasoning performed once and preserved as structural invariants outperforms repeatedly rediscovering the same reasoning). Operator-ratified 2026-07-18.

invariants.statement: Invariant Intelligence does not require machine-discovered invariants; it requires machine-operational invariants. Humans, AI systems, hybrid teams, domain experts, and communities may all contribute structural invariants; the intelligence comes from operating over the invariant substrate, not from who first articulated it.

provenance.source: Operator + Aletheon reflection on the Stage-0 shakedown results (2026-07-18): the hybrid-intelligence thesis clarification. invariants.statement: A resolution engine is an instrument, not the discovery. Structural invariants exist independently of the engines that resolve them — as DNA existed before PCR, and PCR operationalised genetics without discovering it. Instrument-calibration results (coverage against expert baselines) are therefore never evidence for or against the structural thesis; they measure only whether the instrument is good enough to bootstrap a growing corpus.

invariants.statement: The primary source of the invariant corpus is humanity's accumulated reasoning: thousands of years of lived, collective expertise captured as structural invariants so machine intelligence can reuse them to short-circuit and accelerate reasoning. The system's purpose is not to replace expert judgment but to accumulate it — reusing preserved reasoning I(R) is the claim, not out-discovering the experts who produced R.

invariants.statement: Structural-intelligence questions (do invariants exist; does their reuse improve inference), invariant-discovery questions (who or what identifies them best), and constitutional-operation questions (standing, governance, ratification, provenance, delegation) are distinct hypothesis families that must never be entangled. A discovery-calibration result must never be read as structural evidence, and standing expresses operational confidence in using an invariant that already exists — it never creates structural truth.

invariants.statement: The Hybrid Intelligence Thesis: the highest-performing intelligence systems will be neither purely human nor purely artificial, but hybrid systems in which structural invariants provide a shared substrate through which previously validated reasoning is accumulated, operationalised, and reused across both human and machine cognition. Neither replaces the other; each amplifies the other.

Definitional clarifications operator-ratified 2026-07-18; the two hypotheses enter proposed (under test, mirroring inv.reasoning.323). THE central research hypothesis of the Institute — proposed, falsifiable, not validated.

invariants.statement: An invariant is a transferable reasoning primitive that is independent of the intelligence that discovered it. Once discovered and validated, an invariant is no longer human reasoning nor machine reasoning — it becomes part of a shared cognitive substrate both can converge on, contribute to, and reuse.

invariants.statement: Invariants are the common language of hybrid intelligence. Knowledge transfer between human and machine through natural language alone is lossy — much of the reasoning stays implicit; a shared invariant substrate lets both systems operate against the same structure without recreating each other's reasoning. Serialization into a prompt is merely one current transport mechanism: the invariant exists before the prompt and beyond the prompt.

invariants.statement: The division of cognitive labour in hybrid intelligence is an open research question, not an assumption. At the current stage of the programme, human expertise provides the richest known source of validated reasoning from which structural invariants can be extracted; whether humans, machines, or hybrid systems ultimately prove superior at invariant discovery is itself something the programme must test, never presuppose.

The intellectual-honesty guard: preserves the vision without overclaiming discovery superiority for any party.

invariants.statement: The Cumulative Intelligence Hypothesis: civilizations become more intelligent not because individual reasoning continually improves, but because validated reasoning accumulates into an increasingly reusable structural substrate — as DNA lets biological adaptations accumulate across generations, invariant substrates may let reasoning adaptations accumulate across generations of both humans and machines. The analogy is a research framing, not an asserted truth.
