invariants.statement: DidQubes comprise three constitutional classes: the kybe DID — anonymized proof-of-life, with World ID serving as the humanity verification; the root DID — proof of identity, potentially KYC-grade and identifiable; and the persona — the integration and interaction face toward legacy systems and agents. Personas anchor to the root DID, which anchors to the kybe DID.

provenance.source: Operator ratification 2026-07-20 — DidQube three-class model (observer/spine alignment session)

invariants.statement: The Polity Passport is kybe-driven: it is underpinned by the kybe DID and attaches to proven personhood — a level beneath persona. The passport is the anonymized credential of the person, never a property of any single persona.

provenance.source: Operator ratification 2026-07-20 — DidQube three-class model (observer/spine alignment session)

invariants.statement: Citizen-level access rights sit at the personhood level, not the persona level.

provenance.source: Operator ratification 2026-07-20 — DidQube three-class model (observer/spine alignment session)

invariants.statement: The bounded agent is bound to the citizen's passport but acts through their persona, inheriting the identifiability state of that persona.

provenance.source: Operator ratification 2026-07-20 — DidQube three-class model (observer/spine alignment session)

invariants.statement: The person remains anonymous by default; identity is surfaced only via the persona.

provenance.source: Operator ratification 2026-07-20 — DidQube three-class model (observer/spine alignment session)

invariants.statement: Standing accrues to the person, not to the persona. The passport thereby enables continuity of personhood anonymously.

provenance.source: Operator ratification 2026-07-20 — DidQube three-class model (observer/spine alignment session)

invariants.statement: Every credential and state is observed at its own DidQube class: passport and citizen-level access at the personhood level; the delegated agent's acting context and inherited identifiability at the persona level. Flattening observation onto a single class — such as reading a personhood credential through a persona key — is a constitutional violation of the DidQube framework.

provenance.source: Operator ratification 2026-07-20 — DidQube three-class model (observer/spine alignment session)

invariants.statement: Invariants are discovered from evidence, not generated. The discovery engine proposes candidate invariants; it never asserts truth. Discovery, not invention.

provenance.source: CFS-048 Invariant Discovery Engine charter — operator + Aletheon ratification 2026-07-20

invariants.statement: Evidence-first provenance: no candidate invariant exists without traceable provenance to at least one evidence source.

provenance.source: CFS-048 Invariant Discovery Engine charter — operator + Aletheon ratification 2026-07-20

invariants.statement: Invariant discovery is a reasoning-compression problem: the objective is the smallest reusable explanatory structure that accounts for recurring patterns across a domain, not document summarisation.

provenance.source: CFS-048 Invariant Discovery Engine charter — operator + Aletheon ratification 2026-07-20

invariants.statement: A candidate invariant remains proposed until the validation harness supports it; only validated candidates become canonical. Discovery never bypasses validation.

provenance.source: CFS-048 Invariant Discovery Engine charter — operator + Aletheon ratification 2026-07-20

invariants.statement: Invariants are discovered across three classes: constitutional (normative — obligations, rights, governance, compliance), structural (descriptive — conservation, dependency, sequencing, feedback), and experiential (elicited from practitioners). Each is subject to the same validation gate.

provenance.source: CFS-048 Invariant Discovery Engine charter — operator + Aletheon ratification 2026-07-20

invariants.statement: The runtime is itself a discovery source: every execution generates evidence that can challenge, refine, or strengthen the invariant library, closing a self-improving discovery loop.

provenance.source: CFS-048 Invariant Discovery Engine charter — operator + Aletheon ratification 2026-07-20

invariants.statement: Invariants are discovered domain-first: a domain's governing invariants come from that domain's evidence before any cross-domain generalisation, and universality is discovered by comparison across domains, never presupposed.

provenance.source: CFS-048 Phase 1a — methodology correction from the Financial Services first-run (operator + Aletheon 2026-07-20)

invariants.statement: Discovered invariants are scoped on a ladder from domain to sub-domain to capability to task, extending the single-inheritance ontology-class hierarchy; cross-domain and constitutional invariants are the emergent result of recurrence across independently discovered domains, expressed through contexts and edges rather than presupposed parents.

provenance.source: CFS-048 Phase 1a — domain ladder (operator + Aletheon 2026-07-20)

invariants.statement: Cross-framework convergence — the count of independent evidence sources implying a candidate — is a discovery prioritisation signal, not a validity claim; support is evidence, not truth, so broad convergence raises validation priority and never confidence directly.

provenance.source: CFS-048 Phase 1a — convergence signal, respecting Law XII (operator + Aletheon 2026-07-20)

invariants.statement: A domain's governing invariants form a minimum sufficient basis: the smallest validated set that grounds the domain's reasoning at a target fidelity; sufficiency is an experiment, and sub-domain bases refine rather than contradict the domain basis.

provenance.source: CFS-048 Phase 1a — K*_domain framing, instance of IRL-011 §6.3 / P5 (operator + Aletheon 2026-07-20)

invariants.statement: A domain-level invariant is earned by cross-sub-domain compression: its support is the degree to which it recurs independently across sub-domains, not a model's self-reported confidence. The initial domain baseline is a provisional hypothesis set that later sub-domain evidence may support, split, specialize, or expose as incomplete.

provenance.source: CFS-048 Phase 2 Compare — earned domain invariants (operator + Aletheon 2026-07-20)

invariants.statement: Invariant discovery is recursive compression: evidence to domain invariants, domain to cross-domain invariants, and cross-domain to constitutional invariants are distinct compression operations at rising abstraction. Higher-order invariants are earned through repeated independent discovery and comparison, never presupposed a priori.

provenance.source: CFS-048 Phase 2 Compare — recursive-compression methodology (operator + Aletheon 2026-07-20)

invariants.statement: An experiment may generate new hypotheses, but it may not redefine its own success criteria after observation. A result feeds the NEXT protocol; it never rewrites the criteria of the protocol under which it was produced. Post-hoc redefinition of what an experiment was testing is the precise failure mode pre-registration exists to prevent.

provenance.source: Austin-review response + Aletheon actions — experiment-integrity / frozen-protocol discipline (operator-ratified 2026-07-21)

invariants.statement: Every experiment separates three constitutional objects: OBSERVATION (the immutable record of what happened), INTERPRETATION (what the outcome is agreed to mean, frozen at the protocol’s signature), and HYPOTHESIS GENERATION (what to test next, free to evolve). Collapsing interpretation back into observation is post-hoc reinterpretation; keeping the three distinct preserves experimental integrity and research creativity at once — an experiment may inspire the next, but its own observation and interpretation stay fixed once frozen.

provenance.source: Austin-review response + Aletheon actions — experiment-integrity / frozen-protocol discipline (operator-ratified 2026-07-21)

invariants.statement: A validation experiment freezes its judge, its target model, and its interpretation table BEFORE any result exists. Because outcomes move materially across models and judges, an unfrozen instrument measures the instrument rather than the claim; the interpretation table is the one place where the meaning of an outcome is fixed before the outcome exists, and it may not be “improved” mid-stream.

provenance.source: Austin-review response + Aletheon actions — experiment-integrity / frozen-protocol discipline (operator-ratified 2026-07-21)

invariants.statement: A runtime evaluated against the corpus from which it was derived validates INTERNAL COHERENCE, not domain generality. The derived-domain affinity is by design and must be reported as a pre-registered limitation, with domain-independent validation sequenced as a distinct later phase — never conflated with, nor presented as, a claim of generality.

provenance.source: Austin-review response + Aletheon actions — experiment-integrity / frozen-protocol discipline (operator-ratified 2026-07-21)

invariants.statement: Protocol design is the responsibility of the originating research team; the independent reviewer evaluates, challenges, and replicates it — never jointly optimises it before execution. A reviewer recommendation is an input to the design, not an amendment to it: adopting a reviewer’s constraint as a principle is legitimate, but letting the reviewer author the design collapses the independence that makes the review worth having. Reviewer contributions that change the protocol belong to a successor experiment, after the current one concludes.

provenance.source: Aletheon — reviewer-independence / role-separation boundary (operator-ratified 2026-07-21); IRL-016
